title: Unable to start podman systemd service, possibly due to `using API endpoint: ''` 🤷🏽‍♂️
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `sudo systemctl start podman` 
2. `sudo systemctl status podman` 
3. `journalctl --reverse --unit=podman`

**Describe the results you received:**
```
Apr 13 14:49:11 ArcoB systemd[1]: Starting Podman API Service...
Apr 13 14:49:11 ArcoB systemd[1]: Started Podman API Service.
Apr 13 14:49:11 ArcoB podman[916556]: time="2021-04-13T14:49:11-06:00" level=info msg="/usr/bin/podman filtering at log level info"
Apr 13 14:49:11 ArcoB podman[916556]: time="2021-04-13T14:49:11-06:00" level=info msg="Not using native diff for overlay, this may cause degraded performance for building images: kernel has CONFIG_OVERLAY_FS_REDIRECT_DIR enabled"
Apr 13 14:49:11 ArcoB podman[916556]: time="2021-04-13T14:49:11-06:00" level=info msg="Found CNI network podman (type=bridge) at /etc/cni/net.d/87-podman-bridge.conflist"
Apr 13 14:49:11 ArcoB podman[916556]: time="2021-04-13T14:49:11-06:00" level=info msg="Setting parallel job count to 37"
Apr 13 14:49:11 ArcoB podman[916556]: time="2021-04-13T14:49:11-06:00" level=info msg="using systemd socket activation to determine API endpoint"
Apr 13 14:49:11 ArcoB podman[916556]: time="2021-04-13T14:49:11-06:00" level=info msg="using API endpoint: ''"
Apr 13 14:49:11 ArcoB podman[916556]: time="2021-04-13T14:49:11-06:00" level=info msg="API server listening on \"/run/podman/podman.sock\""
Apr 13 14:49:16 ArcoB systemd[1]: podman.service: Deactivated successfully.
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Podman should start

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.1.0
API Version:  3.1.0
Go Version:   go1.16.2
Git Commit:   9f09fb62cba8f44c18eda84db3e72aab3ae76046-dirty
Built:        Wed Mar 31 08:46:48 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**


Yes

comments.body: You have to use the podman.socket not the service. `systemctl start podman.socket`.

@Luap99 
maybe the problem is not there because on my system, `systemctl start service-name` starts both the socket and the service.

```
➜ systemctl is-active podman.socket
inactive
✖ systemctl is-active podman.service
inactive
✖ sudo systemctl start podman
➜ systemctl is-active podman.service
active
➜ systemctl is-active podman.socket 
active
```

We wait a little bit longer...
```
➜ systemctl is-active podman.service
inactive
```

Anyways, it's probably not a big deal. I was just curious as to why it stops, but it looks like all we need is just the socket to be active, not the service
podman is not a daemon. The service has a default timeout of 5 seconds. If you connect to socket systemd will automatically start the service. If the service is currently not in use it will stop.
